Disease Modeling

Marketplace Conference | 27-28 October 2026, Berlin

 
 

Translating Innovation into Real-World Adoption

Join us for our first-ever Marketplace Conference, a dedicated spin-off of the Disease Modeling track at BioCHIP Berlin. The conference brings together researchers, technologists, and industry experts to connect cutting-edge research and innovations from universities, labs, and institutes with industry end-users and prospective partners, fostering collaboration, knowledge transfer, and commercialization of the latest advances in disease modeling.

Interdisciplinary meetup: who should attend

  • Researchers and developers of disease modeling technologies

  • Scientists developing advanced in vitro disease models

  • End-users of disease modeling technologies

  • Providers of disease modeling platforms and enabling technologies

  • Stakeholders driving collaboration, translation and adoption

 

Focal Topics

Advances in organoids, microfluidics, and tissue engineering for disease modelling

  • Organoid systems, organ-on-chip platforms, microfluidic technologies, and tissue engineering approaches for modeling human disease mechanisms, physiology, and drug responses in vitro.

Novel Disease Models Across Human Conditions

  • Disease models spanning oncology, neurology, cardiovascular, metabolic, and rare diseases, including novel and emerging model systems.

Translational Applications of Disease Models

  • In vitro disease models in drug discovery, preclinical testing, biomarker development and precision medicine.

Disease Modeling Products, Platforms, and Services

  • Commercially available and custom-developed solutions including organ-on-chip devices, organoid kits, iPSC-derived and CRISPR-engineered models, and integrated platforms for drug discovery and translational research.

Special Track on Modeling of Tumor Microenvironment

Engineered modeling platforms including organoid, organ-on-chip, microfluidic, and tissue-engineered systems for tumor–immune–stroma reconstruction.
